characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The main character. A talking dog. Afraid of Needles: Is afraid of shots, but not as much as Skits. Bad Liar: Whenever she tells lies, they're not very convincing. She states in "Martha's No Dummy" that it's because she's a dog, and all dogs are bad liars. Big Eater: Has limits, but eats a lot. Bilingual Animal: Martha can talk to humans thanks to her diet of alphabet soup, but she can still understand most animals.

characters.name: Martha
characters.description: The main character. A talking dog. Afraid of Needles: Is afraid of shots, but not as much as Skits. Bad Liar: Whenever she tells lies, they're not very convincing. She states in "Martha's No Dummy" that it's because she's a dog, and all dogs are bad liars. Big Eater: Has limits, but eats a lot. Bilingual Animal: Martha can talk to humans thanks to her diet of alphabet soup, but she can still understand most animals.

characters.name: Helen Lorraine
characters.description: Martha's ten-year-old owner. Animal Lover: Likes dogs, and helped convince Martha that cats aren't all bad. Audience Surrogate: She's a normal girl with an anything but normal dog. Compressed Vice: In "Helen's All Thumbs", she takes up video games but must give them up due to becoming obsessed with them and hallucinating due to them. The video game she played never affected anyone else negatively and Helen has never shown any troubles that would make her that way. Does Not Like Spam: She doesn't like rhubarb pie.

characters.name: Skits
characters.description: The Lorraines' other dog. Afraid of Needles: He is scared of the vet due to getting shots. Big Eater: Again, he has his limits, but still eats a great deal. Big Friendly Dog: Friendly, and bigger than Martha. Cloud Cuckoo Lander: Is a bit of a goof, e.g. he thought the Earth had a yolk like an egg once.

characters.name: Mariella Lorraine
characters.description: Birthday Episode: Her birthday happened in "Martha Runs the Store". Compressed Vice: In "Martha's Dirty Habit", she shows disdain for Danny's between-meal snacking, which she's never done before or since. Gratuitous Spanish: Sometimes puts Spanish words into her sentences. Happily Married: To Danny. They love each other a lot, and even when they come into conflict, they don't argue. In-Series Nickname: Martha calls her "Mom", even though she's clearly not her mom.

characters.name: Daniel "Danny" Lorraine
characters.description: Compressed Abstinence: Tries to give up snacking in "Martha's Dirty Habit", despite having no reason to (not sick, no eating disorders, not overweight etc). Justified because he was trying to cheer Martha (who was trying to abstain from digging) up. Green-Eyed Monster: Was jealous of Mariella and Martha as they were both bilingual, but he wasn't. Happily Married: To Mariella. They love each other a lot and their only negative interaction was her frowning at him once. In-Series Nickname: "Danny", and Martha calls him "Dad" even though he is clearly not Martha's dad. Jack of All Trades: Has several jobs, including sports coach and bus driver.

characters.name: Jake Lorraine
characters.description: Helen's little brother. His age is being described as "a few months shy of two", making him somewhere between 19 and 21 months old. Accidental Misnaming: When he tries to say Martha's name he often misses out the 'h' or the 't'. Baby See, Baby Do: He'll often copy what people say. Baby Talk: Cannot talk properly yet. Canon Foreigner: In the original books, Helen appears to be an only child.

characters.name: Lucille "Lucy" Lorraine
characters.description: Alliterative Name:Lucille"Lucy"Lorraine. Birthday Episode: Her birthday was celebrated in "Martha Says it with Flowers". Does Not Like Spam: She doesn't like bacon. Grumpy Old Woman: She sometimes complains about untidiness, or about Martha's antics. Happily Married: To Bernie. Even though she's grumpy, they get along very well.

characters.name: Bernard "Bernie" Lorraine
characters.description: Birthday Episode: His birthday was celebrated in "Martha's Worst Best Day". Collector of the Strange: Much like T.D., he is a pack rat and has a collection of random, unrelated items. Cool Old Guy: Has a playful, happy-go-lucky personality. He's also elderly. In-Series Nickname: Martha sometimes calls him Grandpa or Granddad despite him not being her grandfather.

characters.name: Jorge
characters.description: Birthday Episode: It was his birthday "Martha's Market". No Full Name Given: His last name, which would be Mariella's maiden name, is unknown. Pretentious Pronunciation: Pronounces "chocolate" as "choco-la-tay". Shared Family Quirks: He and his sister Mariella both cook. Supreme Chef: He's renowned for his recipes; he made a whole feast when he came over in "?Que Pasa, Martha?".

characters.name: Carolina
characters.description: Helen's eleven-year-old cousin. Ambiguously Absent Parent: Her mother is never seen or mentioned. Dumb Struck: When she has stage fright, she can't even speak. Fantastic Racism: Not so much anymore, but she used to have prejudice against dogs. The Fashionista: Is serious about clothes. She often tries on new clothes and judges other people's clothes.

characters.name: T.D. Kennelly
characters.description: Afraid of Blood:Possibly.Helen once claimed hepassed outwhen he saw Martha bleeding, though it's unknown if that was true. Attention Deficit... Ooh, Shiny!: Downplayed, but he's a bit easily distracted. Cloud Cuckoo Lander: A bit; he has a vivid imagination and often thinks up crazy stuff. Collector of the Strange: Keeps random odds and ends at the junkyard. Genius Ditz: Is a bit of aCloudcuckoolander, but is actually a very smart kid.

characters.name: O.G. Kennelly
characters.description: Cloud Cuckoo Lander: He's a bit of a goofball, inventing weird things and having bizarre notions like his son. Gadgeteer Genius: Is an inventor. Genius Ditz: Goofy but at the same time highly intelligent. Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: Just like his son, he's blond and kind. Like Father, Like Son: Both he and T.D. have blond hair and are goofy and inventive.

characters.name: Janice Kennelly
characters.description: T.D.'s mother and the vice-principal of the school. Amazingly Embarrassing Parent: Embarrasses T.D., which is odd because T.D. is supposed tonotget embarrassed. Neat Freak: Doesn't like it when O.G. and T.D. mess up the house with their inventions. She also has aRunning Gagof having remind T.D to do his chores and doesn't like that T.D keeps his room a mess. Only Sane Woman: Tries to make sure O.G. and T.D. don't get too "out of control".

characters.name: C.K.
characters.description: No Full Name Given: His last name is unstated, but it's probably "Kennelly" since going by one's initials seems to be on O.G.'s side of the family, making him probably either O.G.'s brother or father. Retcon: Early episodes refer to him as T.D's uncle, while later episodes suddenly have him being referred to as T.D's grandfather. This change is never addressed by anyone.

characters.name: C.D.
characters.description: And Call Him "George": While he hasn't killed any dogs, heisa "doggie lover" (dogs' term for someone who hugs dogs too tightly) and has to learn how to behave around them. Birthday Episode: He's four in his first appearance and turns five in "T.D.'s Magic". Genki Boy: When he's not scared or sad, he's often running around and shouting gleefully. The Klutz: The reason why Martha's scared of him: he knocks her over when he hugs her, and squeezes her too hard. Mood-Swinger: Due to his young age, he goes from happy to sad and back easily.

characters.name: Alice Boxwood
characters.description: Animal Lover: Likes animals as much as the other kids. Genki Girl: Is very energetic. Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: Kind (for instance, "Alice Tells a Story" has her adopt Nelson out of pity because she thinks no one else would adopt him due to his meanness), and blonde. The Klutz: She's very clumsy, often tripping, dropping things, etc. Limited Wardrobe: Which consists of glasses, brown pants, red shoes, and a green T-shirt with a purple stripe.

characters.name: Ronald Boxwood
characters.description: Alice's teenage brother. Big Brother Bully: Takes pleasure out of annoying Alice, the other kids, and the dogs, too. The Bully: Not only annoys Alice but annoys the other children too. Deadpan Snarker: One of his traits which the other characters don't like is his habit of making snide remarks. Gadgeteer Genius: Likes computers and whatnot.

characters.name: Mr. and Mrs. Boxwood
characters.description: Gadgeteer Genius: Both very interested in technology. Good Parents: Seem to want what's best for both their kids. No Full Name Given: Neither Mr. nor Mrs. Boxwood has a known first name. Shared Family Quirks: They share their son's interest in technology.

characters.name: Nelson
characters.description: The Bully: Bullies Martha and Skits a lot. Cats Are Mean: Is a cat and mean. However, he's the only mean cat in the series. Even Bullies Have Standards: Nelson is arrogant and rude, but in "Martha vs. Robot", he's horrified at the idea of Ronald throwing awayDynamo, since the Boxwoods consider him just as much of a pet as Nelson and Polly. Never My Fault: Always tries to pin the blame on Martha. The Rival: To Martha and Skits; they're often competing against each other.

characters.name: Polly
characters.description: Stock Animal Name: Her name is "Polly", which is probably the most generic parrot name in the world. Vitriolic Best Buds: Martha and Polly seem to get along pretty well, but Martha does sometimes insult Polly.

characters.name: Truman Oatley
characters.description: Black and Nerdy: He is African-American and also has an unquenchable thirst for knowledge. Book Worm: Reads a lot. Deadpan Snarker: He has his moments. Dork in a Sweater: He wears a sweater vest and is a timid bookworm. Green-Eyed Monster: Was jealous of the babies in the episode "Martha Plays Favourites"

characters.name: Mrs. Oatley
characters.description: Friend to All Children: The reason why she runs a daycare is she likes kids. No Full Name Given: Her first name is unspoken.

characters.name: Mr. Oatley
characters.description: No Full Name Given: We don't know his first name.

characters.name: Milo Lee
characters.description: The seven-year-old best friend of Truman. Asian and Nerdy: Downplayed — he seems to be Asian, or at least half-Asian judging by his and his father's last name and appearance, and "Dinosaurs in Trouble" shows that he's into palaeontology. Keet: He's about as active as Alice. Lovable Coward: Is a bit nervy, but not as much as Truman. Missing Mom: Lives only with his dad; his mother is never metioned at all.

characters.name: Terrence Lee
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: He's often shown to have bad luck — the movers keep breaking his things in "Skits and Mr. Scruffles", and he struggles to buy a pillow in "Martha's Canine Cleaners".

characters.name: Mrs. Demson
characters.description: Helen's neighbour, who's a grumpy and shrewish old woman. Cranky Neighbour: Complains a lot and lives near the Lorraines and Oatleys. Fantastic Racism: Against dogs. She blames them for everything. Fun-Hating Confiscating Adult: Whatever toys get stuck on her roof stay there. Grumpy Old Woman: An old woman who's extremely snappy and negative.

characters.name: Kazuo
characters.description: The animal shelter owner. Ambiguously Brown: Has a lighter brown skin like Carolina's. Animal Lover: Fits his job — he runs an animal shelter because he wants what's best for the animals. Comically Missing the Point: He tells Martha to bark once for one dog food and twice for the other, forgetting Martha can talk. Later, he says that he found a talking dog but he's not sure if she'sthattalking dog. Nice Guy: Very kind; he does his darnedest to help the animals.

characters.name: Pops
characters.description: Cloud Cuckoo Lander: A bit of a clown, he sometimes rolls over. Cool Old Guy: Is an elderly dog and good friends with the other dogs. The Quiet One: Doesn't bark as much as his friends.

characters.name: The Cluskys
characters.description: Helen, TD, and Alice's teacher. Brainy Brunette: A smart teacher with brown hair. Dork in a Sweater: An academic who wears a blue sweater. No Full Name Given: Her full name isn't said. Perpetual Frowner: Despite being a serious teacher, she hardly ever smiles.

characters.name: The Parkingtons
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: Mr. Parkington is quite sassy. Lovable Coward: Mrs. Parkington has moments of nerviness and so does John. Mood-Swinger: John. One minute he's neurotic and the next, he's happy as can be. No Full Name Given: We don't know their first name. Nosy Neighbor: Mrs. Parkington is quite nosy, but her husband generally isn't.

characters.name: Bob
characters.description: Angry Guard Dog: Zigzags between this andBig Friendly Dog. He occasionally growls and chases everyone who comes near his property, but other times, he just wants to make friends. Big Friendly Dog: Zigzags between this and Angry Guard Dog. He occasionally growls and chases everyone who comes near his property, but other times, he just wants to make friends. Berserk Button: Donotcall him a bad dog or play a high-pitched frequency near him. Cloud Cuckoo Lander: Martha notes he's strange because he "plays with his steaks and eats his toys". Extreme Omnivore: Once tried to eat his toy.

characters.name: Teddy
characters.description: Big Friendly Dog: Is large and friendly to other dogs, people and cats. The Ditz: Known for being stupid; hating rain, not being able to carry sticks through doggie doors, and being unable to play catch. Extreme Omnivore: Eats anything from diapers to pillows to rocks. This made him sick in "The Trouble With Teddy" and he needed an operation. The Klutz: Bumps into things a lot. Sick Episode: He gets sick in "The Trouble With Teddy".

characters.name: Burt
characters.description: Plot Allergy: Is allergic to his flea collar. In fact, it makes him puke.

characters.name: Leon
characters.description: Big Friendly Dog: Is one of the larger dogs and friendly.

characters.name: Slappy
characters.description: The Eeyore: It doesn't take much to make her sad. She once got sad because her water bowl was too low. Sleepyhead: Likes to nap.

characters.name: Rinty
characters.description: Big Friendly Dog: A sociable, easy-going Alsatian.

characters.name: Pablum and Otis Weaselgraft
characters.description: Two con men, who are almost always trying to steal Martha. Bald of Evil: Weaselgraft is a bald villain. Con Man: Both like to scam people. Corrupted Character Copy: They are this to Carl and Major Monogram respectively. As both Pablum and Carl wear glasses, are redheads and serve as the interns to their respective bosses. Both Otis Weaselgraft and Major Monogram wear green outfits, boss their interns around and believe in Santa. But while Carl and Monogram are good hearted and just want to keep an organization they know about a secret especially their secret agent platypus named Perry, Pablum and Otis are con men who want to steal the dog named Martha so they can exploit her information on how she learned to speak. The Dragon: Pablum seems to be Weaselgraft's sidekick.

characters.name: Reginald Steinglass
characters.description: Ambiguously Jewish: His last name, Steinglass, is common among Ashkenazi Jews. Dork in a Sweater: Wears a sweater vest and says big words. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Like Ronald, he's a bit of a bully, but actually has a soft spot. Jewish and Nerdy: Implied. He says big words and his last name makes himAmbiguously Jewish. Lean and Mean: Like Ronald, he's skinny and likes messing with the younger kids.

characters.name: Mr. Stern
characters.description: Meaningful Name: He yells a lot and chases Martha away. Neat Freak: Makes a big deal over even a hair out of place. No Full Name Given: We don't know his first name.

characters.name: Granny Flo
characters.description: Greed: In "Martha Blah Blah", she lays off employees to make money. No Full Name Given: Her last name is unsaid. Scratchy-Voiced Senior: She has a high, croaky voice. When I Was Your Age...: Mentions how in her childhood, alphabet soup didn't have the entire alphabet.

characters.name: Joe
characters.description: Added Alliterative Appeal: His nickname Junkyard Joe. Green Aesop: He likes to invoke this trope by teaching people about garbage. In-Series Nickname: Junkyard Joe is his main nickname. No Full Name Given: His last name is unrevealed.

characters.name: Courageous Collie Carlo
characters.description: Alliterative Name: Three "C"s. Broken Pedestal: Becomes this briefly in"Starstruck Martha"by the human fans when they find out his fear of flowers. They also hate him further when they don't fall for a fake bear scheme planned by Martha, Truman and Helen. Fortunately, he becomes aRebuilt Pedestalafter planning a scheme with arealbear named Bobo. The Danza:In-Universe. His name is "Carlo", which is the same name is his character. Lovable Coward: On TV he is brave, but he is actually timid. Plot Allergy: He has hay fever, which led to him causing a mishap by sneezing.

characters.name: Kitten
characters.description: The Bus Came Back: Returns later on the series to teach Martha how to become a cat in a play. Cute Kitten: Is a very sweet little kitten. A Dog Named "Dog": A kitten named Kitten. Morality Pet: Literally, as he is a pet and he helped Martha realise thatCats Are Meandoes not always apply. Ridiculously Cute Critter: His role is to be cute.

characters.name: Jeffy
characters.description: Gentle Giant: He is a big elephant who is sweet and bashful. Shrinking Violet: Is a bit shy.

characters.name: Professor Monkey
characters.description: Einstein Hair: He is a really smart monkey with a hairstyle reminiscent of Albert Einstein. Formally Named Pet: A pet monkey namedProfessor. Half-Dressed Cartoon Animal: Wears only a lab coat. Labcoat of Science and Medicine: A genius monkey in a lab coat. Species Surname: Doesn't have a first name, but his title is "Professor" and his surname is "Monkey".

characters.name: Bethyl
characters.description: No Full Name Given: We don't know what her last name is. Satellite Character: Usually seen in episodes with Professor Monkey.

characters.name: Windy Mc Cloud
characters.description: Punny Name: A weather lady with "wind" and "cloud" in her name.

characters.name: Mr. Hucky
characters.description: No Full Name Given: We don't know his first name.

characters.name: Boomer
characters.description: Blind Without 'Em: Seems to be either very nearsighted or has an astigmatism, since he can barely see and is prone to makingBlind Mistakeswithout his glasses. Talk Like a Pirate: He is an old sailor who talks as if he were a pirate.

characters.name: Ralph
characters.description: Animal Gender-Bender: Is a female duck, as revealed in "The Return of Ralph", but has a green head like a drake. Gender-Blender Name: Is actually female despite her name. Nearly Normal Animal: Is a relatively normal duck, but she quacks to a tune once. Shared Family Quirks: She and her kids all like to watch TV. Your Tomcat Is Pregnant: Was thought to be male at first, but later revealed to be female when she laid eggs.
